findings.issue: «تشبيهات قرآنية» bucket is ~51% false positives: the «كاف التشبيه» heuristic fires on the generic connective particles «كذلك» (thus/likewise) and «كما» (as/just as, in the sense of manner-of-action) merely because QAC tags their prefixed كَ as pos=P — with no check on what kind of word the كَ attaches to.
findings.evidence: js/scripts/export-amthal.mjs:31-40 builds `kaf` from `s0.role==="prefix" && s0.pos==="P" && norm(s0.text)==="ك"` with no check on the STEM segment (segs[1]). Live count: 235 unique ayahs match this rule; word-form breakdown shows كذلك=86 and كما=59 of 247 matched words. I computed the exact overlap against the final similes[] array (257 entries): 130/257 (50.6%) are present ONLY because of a bare كذلك/كما and contain no كمثل/كأن/substantive كاف-noun at all — e.g. 2:73 «فَقُلْنَا ٱضْرِبُوهُ بِبَعْضِهَا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يُحْىِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ» and 2:151 «كَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا فِيكُمْ رَسُولًا» — neither is a rhetorical simile. Root cause identified via quran-kg.db segment table: the stem after كَ has pos="DEM" for كذلك and pos="SUB" for كما (both pos_basic values differ), whereas genuine similes (كصيّب، كمثل، كشجرة) and legitimate implicit comparisons (كمن، كالذي — pos="REL") have a real nominal stem. Excluding stems with pos in {DEM,SUB} cuts similes from 257→129 cleanly, removing exactly the noise while preserving all substantive comparisons.

findings.issue: The «مضروبة» (parables) vs «تشبيهات» (similitudes) split is a linguistic accident (does root ضرب happen to co-occur with root مثل in the exact same ayah?), not the classical genre distinction. Several of the Qur'an's most famous مثل مصرّح (canonical parables) are consequently mislabeled as mere «تشبيهات».
findings.evidence: Checked roots directly: 29:41 (العنكبوت/spider — «مَثَلُ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا... كَمَثَلِ الْعَنكَبُوتِ»), 62:5 (donkey carrying books), 7:176 (the dog), 2:17 (fire-kindler) all contain root مثل but NOT root ضرب in that ayah — so export-amthal.mjs:27-28,43 puts them in similes, not parables. A reader browsing «أمثالٌ مضروبة» in Amthal.tsx:101-105 will never see the spider, the dog, or the donkey there; they appear only under «تشبيهاتٌ قرآنية» (Amthal.tsx:106-110), whose own subtitle text says «تشبيهٌ يُقرِّب المعنى بنظيرٍ محسوس» (a mere rhetorical comparison) — understating that these ARE full didactic أمثال.

findings.issue: Completeness gap: 91 of the 148 ayahs carrying root مثل are absent from the page altogether, and at least one canonical مثل مصرّح is missing entirely — 13:35 «مَّثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ الَّتِى وُعِدَ الْمُتَّقُونَ» (the Paradise parable), because it uses bare «مثل» (no ضرب co-occurrence, no «كمثل», no كاف/كأن marker).
findings.evidence: Computed set difference: mithlAyahs(148) − (parables∪similes from amthal.json) = 91 locs, including 13:35. Its near-twin 47:15 (same opening «مَّثَلُ الْجَنَّةِ...») IS captured, but only incidentally via the unrelated comparison «كَمَنْ هُوَ خَالِدٌ فِى النَّارِ» later in the same ayah — an arbitrary inclusion/exclusion asymmetry between two parallel verses that shows the marker approach is fragile for the «مصرّحة» category (most of the 91 excluded ones are legitimately non-parabolic uses of «مثل» meaning "like/equal-to", e.g. 2:23 «بسورة من مثله», 4:11 share-language — but 13:35 is a clean miss of a top-tier classical مثل).

findings.issue: Multi-ayah parables are shown as a single trigger verse, with the actual narrative/content of the parable never displayed — the section shows only the "topic sentence", not the parable itself.
findings.evidence: 18:32 (two-gardens parable, «وَٱضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا رَّجُلَيْنِ...») is present in parables[], but 18:33 through 18:44 — the dialogue, the ruin of the garden, the moral — are absent from the page entirely (checked all 13 locs, none present). Same for 36:13 (people of the town, «وَٱضْرِبْ لَهُم مَّثَلًا أَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلْقَرْيَةِ»): 36:14-32 (the messengers, the martyr, the destruction) are entirely absent. A user reading the parable card sees one clause and a link into the reader, not the parable's substance.

findings.issue: No pairing of any مثل with an explanation of its مضرِب (what it illustrates) or مورد (occasion) — the task's explicit ask — even though the codebase already has two ready-made resources for this that go entirely unused by Amthal.tsx.
findings.evidence: Amthal.tsx:59-64 renders only `arName(loc)` + `highlightVerse(texts.get(loc)?.textUthmani, q)` + a Link to the reader — no explanatory text anywhere. Yet js/apps/studio/src/books.ts already exposes an attributed, verse-anchored tafsir lookup (`bookTextAt(source, loc)`, `tafsirFor(loc)`, used today only by TafsirChip.tsx) over 24 cited tafsir books in js/data/tafsir/*.jsonl (muyassar, saadi, jalalayn, ibnkathir, …). Separately, quran-kg.db's `ayah_tafsil` table already has a `rel='مثال'` relation (1047 rows, 937 with review='confirm') linking abstract "hub" ayahs to illustrative example-ayahs — e.g. tafsil_ayah_id for 16:75 (the owned-slave parable) is linked (rel='مثال', review='confirm') to hub ayahs 29:62 and 43:32 about God apportioning provision — precisely the "what does this parable illustrate" relation the task asks for. Only 43/286 amthal.json locs overlap with مثال-tagged tafsil ayahs today (it's a broader concept than literary أمثال), but it's a real, already-built, reviewed asset that is not wired into this section at all.

findings.issue: export-amthal.mjs reads the legacy `quran-app.db` (monlite doc-store schema, table `words` with a denormalized `segments` JSON blob) rather than the canonical `quran-kg.db` (relational `word`/`segment`/`root` tables) that the rest of the KG pipeline (ayah_tafsil, ayah_principle, root_meaning, embeddings) is built on.
findings.evidence: export-amthal.mjs:16 `const DB = path.join(ROOT, "quran-app.db")`. Row counts happen to match today (both report 77,429 words / 6,236 ayahs), so results are currently consistent, but this script is the only one in the «الموضوعات» nav group depending on the old DB — any future divergence between the two DBs (e.g. a QAC correction applied to one but not the other) would silently desync أمثال from the rest of the KG-derived content.

findings.issue: No category exists at all for «الأمثال الكامنة» (implicit parables — didactic comparisons that never use the lemma مثل, e.g. proverbialized ayat), which classical أمثال-القرآن literature treats as a standard third class alongside مصرّحة and تشبيهات بالكاف.
findings.evidence: Amthal.tsx only exposes `kind: "all" | "parables" | "similes"` (line 29) and export-amthal.mjs has no code path for مثل-less implicit comparisons at all — the concept is simply not represented, and can't be, purely deterministically, since implicit parables are identified by classical scholarly judgment rather than a lexical marker.

how_it_works:
أمثال القرآن (route /amthal, nav group «الموضوعات») is built by js/scripts/export-amthal.mjs, a fully deterministic script — no tafsir, no LLM judgment — that reads the legacy `quran-app.db` (`words` table: surahNo/ayahNo/textClean/root/segments-JSON) and writes js/apps/studio/public/amthal.json with two flat arrays of "s:a" locations:
• parables (مضروبة, 29 verses): ayahs where BOTH root ض-ر-ب and root م-ث-ل occur together (export-amthal.mjs:27-28,43).
• similes (تشبيهات, 257 verses): ayahs matching ANY of — a word starting with «كمثل» (line 29), a word starting with «كأن» (line 30), or a word whose first morphological segment is a كَ-prefix tagged as a preposition (لines 31-40, the «كاف التشبيه» heuristic) — minus anything already counted as a parable (lines 44-45).

The two categories were built by the SAME script in the SAME provenance/methodology across two commits: db363ba (2026-07-11) shipped parables(29)+similes(14, كمثل-only); 9f722d9 (2026-07-12) broadened the similes definition to add the كاف-prefix heuristic + كأن, growing similes 14→257. So the owner's "history of adding altashbih" did not introduce a different data pipeline or a model-judged layer — it's the identical deterministic script with a much looser (and, as shown below, much noisier) pattern rule for the second bucket.

Amthal.tsx fetches amthal.json, renders two `group()` sections (parables/similes) each just listing arName(loc) + the single ayah's highlighted text + a Link into the reader (Amthal.tsx:48-68, 101-110) — no explanation, no مضرِب/مورد pairing, no ayah-range for multi-verse parables, filtered only by PageSearch/fuzzyMatch over the verse text and surah name.

proposals.title: Fix كاف-التشبيه precision using the stem's real POS (DEM/SUB exclusion)
proposals.rationale: Verified fix: exclude كاف-prefix hits whose following stem segment has pos='DEM' (كذلك) or pos='SUB' (كما) — both already present as a field in quran-app.db's segments JSON, no DB migration needed. This removes exactly the 128-130 noise entries (257→129) while preserving all substantive comparisons (كصيّب، كمثل، كشجرة) and legitimate relative-pronoun comparisons (كمن، كالذي, pos='REL', kept). One-line change to the existing loop in export-amthal.mjs:31-40, then rerun + commit amthal.json.

proposals.title: Re-tier into three classical categories: مصرّحة / كامنة / تشبيهات بالكاف, with a swarm-assisted classification + owner review for the parts that can't be pure regex
proposals.rationale: مصرّحة (explicit, uses lemma مثل/أمثال as the noun of comparison) needs a semantic pass — not every root-مثل ayah is a parable (e.g. 4:11 share-language vs 13:35 Paradise parable) — so run a claude-swarm classification over the 148 root-مثل ayahs (same pattern already used for ayah_principle/ayah_tafsil: `source='claude-swarm-pass-x'` + `review` column) to tag مصرّح-parable vs not, surfacing misses like 13:35. كامنة (implicit, no مثل lemma at all) genuinely requires scholarly judgment — seed candidates from the EXISTING ayah_tafsil rel='مثال' relation (937 confirmed rows) plus a targeted swarm pass, with an explicit review gate before shipping, exactly like the project's existing ayah_principle convention. تشبيهات بالكاف stays deterministic (fixed heuristic above).

proposals.title: Show each parable's full ayah range, not just the trigger verse
proposals.rationale: 18:32 (two gardens) and 36:13 (people of the town) currently show only the topic sentence; the actual parable content (18:33-44, 36:14-32) never appears. Add a small curated `{start, end}` override table for the ~30-70 multi-ayah entries (trivial size, hand-built once with tafsir cross-check, or LLM-assisted boundary detection + owner spot-check for speed) and render the full span in the reader-linked card instead of a single ayah.

proposals.title: Pair every مثل/تشبيه with an attributed مضرِب explanation, reusing existing data — no new data pipeline needed
proposals.rationale: Wire in what's already built: (a) reuse `bookTextAt`/`tafsirFor` from books.ts (already powering TafsirChip.tsx) to show one short, attributed tafsir excerpt per parable ayah (default muyassar, same UX pattern already shipped elsewhere); (b) where a تفصيل link exists (`ayah_tafsil` rel='مثال', review!='reject'), surface "هذا مثالٌ على⟶ hub ayah" as a direct pointer to the abstract principle it illustrates — this is real, reviewed data already in quran-kg.db, just never queried by this export script. A short swarm pass to condense the (often multi-paragraph, multi-ayah) tafsir excerpt into a one-line مضرِب label per parable would raise this from "raw tafsir dump" to genuinely useful presentation, matching the project's "presentation over data volume" principle.

proposals.title: Migrate export-amthal.mjs from legacy quran-app.db to canonical quran-kg.db
proposals.rationale: Every other پipeline in الموضوعات/الكلّيّات reads quran-kg.db's relational word/segment/root tables (which also expose pos more cleanly for proposal #1's fix, without JSON-parsing a blob per word). Counts match today (77,429 words / 6,236 ayahs in both) so this is low-risk tech-debt cleanup, not a data fix, but removes a silent-desync risk and lets this script benefit from any future QAC corrections applied only to quran-kg.db.

verdict: The section is honestly labeled as computed-from-text-not-tafsir and the "مضروبة" (29) bucket is clean and correctly verified — every entry genuinely contains «ضرب … مثلاً». But the "تشبيهات" bucket (257, added in a later commit using the same deterministic script, not a different provenance) is roughly half noise: the كاف-التشبيه heuristic fires on the generic particles كذلك/كما with no check on what they attach to, which I traced to a precise, fixable root cause in the QAC POS tags already present in the data (DEM/SUB stems vs real nominal stems). Beyond precision, the section conflates the classical مصرّحة/كامنة/تشبيه taxonomy into an accidental two-way split driven by whether ضرب happens to co-occur in the same ayah — so some of the Qur'an's most iconic parables (spider, dog, donkey) are shown as mere "similitudes," one canonical parable (13:35, Paradise) is missing outright, and multi-ayah parables (two gardens, people of the town) are truncated to a single topic sentence with their actual content never shown. Most importantly for the task's own ask: there is no مضرِب/مورد pairing at all today, even though the codebase already has both an attributed 24-book tafsir layer (books.ts) and a reviewed تفصيل layer with a ready-made rel='مثال' relation (937 confirmed rows) that were never wired into this view. All of this is fixable without inventing new infrastructure — a small deterministic fix, a modest curated-range table, and reuse of two data assets that already exist in the codebase.
